steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content:
Sentence 1 makes a general claim: Navarro and Hayes's research was "extremely influential in the fight against CFCs." Sentence 2 provides a concrete example of that influence: it "laid the foundation for a 1987 treaty that phased out the use of CFCs across the globe." The blank transition must signal that a specific example elaborates on the general claim.
  • Choice A — Regardless,: Signals that sentence 2 is true despite sentence 1 — a contrast/concession relationship that does not fit here. ✗
  • Choice B — Specifically,: Signals that sentence 2 gives precise detail elaborating on the general claim of sentence 1, which is exactly the relationship. ✓
  • Choice C — However,: Signals contrast or contradiction, but sentence 2 supports, not contradicts, sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice D — Earlier,: Signals that sentence 2 happened before sentence 1, but the 1987 treaty came after the 1974 research — the opposite of "earlier." ✗

steps.title: Confirm the direction of the elaboration
steps.content: The general-to-specific move (claim then evidence) is the core structure: "extremely influential" is the broad assertion, and the 1987 treaty is the specific proof. "Specifically" is the standard transition for exactly this pattern. That matches Choice B — Specifically,.
